tagline: Estimate how long it takes for your body to eliminate alcohol based on drinks consumed
keywords: BAC elimination, alcohol metabolism, sober up time, alcohol elimination rate

faq.question: How fast does the body eliminate alcohol?
faq.answer: The average person eliminates alcohol at about 0.015% BAC per hour (roughly one standard drink per hour). This rate is relatively constant and cannot be sped up by coffee, food, or cold showers.

faq.question: What is a standard drink?
faq.answer: One standard drink contains 14 grams of pure alcohol: 12 oz beer (5%), 5 oz wine (12%), or 1.5 oz spirits (40%). Craft beers, large glasses, and strong cocktails may count as 1.5-3 standard drinks.

faq.question: Why do women metabolize alcohol differently?
faq.answer: Women typically have a lower water-to-body-weight ratio (r=0.55 vs 0.68 for men) and less of the enzyme alcohol dehydrogenase. This means the same number of drinks produces a higher BAC in women.

formulas: BAC = (Alcohol in grams ÷ (Body Weight kg × r × 10)) - (0.015 × hours drinking × 0.5)

tagline: Estimate cumulative hearing damage risk from noise exposure over time
keywords: hearing loss, noise exposure, decibel damage, hearing protection, NIOSH

faq.question: At what level does noise cause hearing damage?
faq.answer: NIOSH recommends limiting exposure to 85 dB for 8 hours. Every 3 dB increase halves the safe exposure time: 88 dB = 4 hours, 91 dB = 2 hours, 100 dB = 15 minutes, 115 dB = 30 seconds.

faq.question: How effective is hearing protection?
faq.answer: Real-world protection is about 50% of the Noise Reduction Rating (NRR) minus 7 dB. So NRR 30 earplugs provide about (30-7)×0.5 = 11.5 dB of real reduction. Proper fit is critical.

faq.question: Is hearing loss from noise permanent?
faq.answer: Yes. Noise-induced hearing loss (NIHL) is permanent and irreversible. The hair cells in the inner ear do not regenerate. Tinnitus (ringing) is often the first warning sign.

formulas: Every 3 dB doubles the sound energy and halves safe time

tagline: Calculate your daily water intake needs based on weight, activity, and climate
keywords: hydration calculator, water intake, daily water needs, how much water

faq.question: How much water should I drink daily?
faq.answer: A common baseline is half your body weight in ounces (a 160 lb person = 80 oz). Active people, hot climates, and caffeine/alcohol consumption increase needs. Most adults need 64-128 oz (2-4 liters) daily.

faq.question: Does caffeine dehydrate you?
faq.answer: Mildly. Caffeine is a mild diuretic, but the water in coffee/tea mostly offsets this. Add about 4 extra ounces of water per caffeinated drink to fully compensate.

faq.question: What are signs of dehydration?
faq.answer: Mild: dark yellow urine, thirst, dry mouth. Moderate: headache, fatigue, dizziness. Severe: rapid heartbeat, confusion, no urination. Pale/light yellow urine indicates good hydration.

formulas: Adjusted = Base × Activity Factor × Climate Factor

tagline: Estimate vitamin D production from sun exposure based on skin type, time, and location
keywords: vitamin D, sun exposure, UV calculator, vitamin D from sun, sunlight calculator

faq.question: How much sun do I need for vitamin D?
faq.answer: Most people need 10-30 minutes of midday sun on arms and face, 2-3 times per week. Fair-skinned people need less time; darker skin tones need more. UV index must be above 3 for meaningful production.

faq.question: Can I get vitamin D through a window?
faq.answer: No. Glass blocks UVB rays, which are needed for vitamin D synthesis. You must be outdoors or in direct, unfiltered sunlight. UVA rays (which cause aging) do pass through glass.

faq.question: Does sunscreen prevent vitamin D production?
faq.answer: SPF 30 blocks about 97% of UVB rays, significantly reducing vitamin D production. Get your brief unprotected sun exposure first, then apply sunscreen for extended time outdoors.

formulas: Vitamin D ≈ Base Rate × (UV Index ÷ 6) × (Skin Exposed % ÷ 25) × Minutes
Base rates vary by skin type (1000 IU/session for Type I to 150 for Type VI)
Burn Time ≈ Base Burn Time × (6 ÷ UV Index)
Optimal exposure = 50% of burn time, max 30 minutes

tagline: Track and score your allergy symptoms to monitor severity and treatment effectiveness
keywords: allergy score, symptom tracker, allergy severity, TNSS, allergy symptoms

faq.question: What is the TNSS allergy score?
faq.answer: The Total Nasal Symptom Score (TNSS) rates four nasal symptoms (sneezing, congestion, runny nose, itchy nose) from 0-3 each. It's used in clinical trials and by allergists to track treatment effectiveness.

faq.question: When should I see an allergist?
faq.answer: See an allergist if: symptoms persist more than 2 weeks, over-the-counter medications aren't helping, symptoms affect sleep or daily activities, or you have complications like sinus infections or asthma.

faq.question: How do I track allergy severity over time?
faq.answer: Score your symptoms at the same time daily (morning is typical). Track scores over weeks to identify patterns, triggers, and medication effectiveness. A consistent score drop of 30%+ indicates effective treatment.

formulas: TNSS = Sneezing + Congestion + Runny Nose + Itchy Eyes + Itchy Throat (0-3 each)
Quality of Life Score = Sleep Impact + Activity Impact (0-3 each)

tagline: Find your Chinese zodiac animal, element, and compatibility based on birth year
keywords: Chinese zodiac, Chinese astrology, zodiac animal, birth year animal, zodiac compatibility

faq.question: What is the Chinese zodiac?
faq.answer: The Chinese zodiac is a 12-year cycle, with each year represented by an animal: Rat, Ox, Tiger, Rabbit, Dragon, Snake, Horse, Goat, Monkey, Rooster, Dog, and Pig. Your birth year determines your sign.

faq.question: What do the five elements mean?
faq.answer: Each zodiac year is also associated with one of five elements: Metal, Water, Wood, Fire, or Earth. The element adds nuance to your personality. The full cycle (12 animals × 5 elements) repeats every 60 years.

faq.question: Is Chinese zodiac based on the solar or lunar calendar?
faq.answer: The Chinese zodiac follows the lunar calendar. Chinese New Year falls between January 21 and February 20. If born in January or early February, you may belong to the previous year's animal.

formulas: Animal = Birth Year mod 12 (mapped to animal cycle)
Element = Birth Year mod 10 (mapped to element cycle)
Compatibility based on traditional trine groupings

tagline: Calculate the mathematical probability of drawing specific tarot cards or combinations
keywords: tarot probability, tarot odds, card probability, tarot math, tarot statistics

faq.question: What are the odds of drawing a specific tarot card?
faq.answer: In a 78-card deck, the probability of drawing one specific card in a single draw is 1/78 = 1.28%. In a 3-card spread, it's about 3.85%. For the Celtic Cross (10 cards), it's about 12.8%.

faq.question: What are the odds of drawing all Major Arcana in a spread?
faq.answer: For a 3-card spread from a full 78-card deck: the probability of all 3 being Major Arcana is about 2.1%. For a 10-card Celtic Cross spread, it drops to roughly 0.0001%.

faq.question: Does the math disprove tarot readings?
faq.answer: The math shows probabilities of random draws. Tarot practitioners view the cards as tools for intuition and self-reflection, not random predictions. The value lies in interpretation, not statistical probability.

formulas: P(specific card in spread) = C(target, target) × C(remaining, other) / C(deck, drawn)
With reversals: each card has 2 orientations, doubling possibilities
P(all Major Arcana) = C(22, drawn) / C(78, drawn)

tagline: Calculate your real take-home pay from side hustles after taxes and expenses
keywords: side hustle income, gig economy tax, freelance income, side job calculator

faq.question: Do I have to pay self-employment tax on side hustle income?
faq.answer: Yes, if you earn more than $400/year from self-employment. Self-employment tax is 15.3% (12.4% Social Security + 2.9% Medicare) on net earnings. You can deduct half of this on your income tax.

faq.question: What expenses can I deduct from side hustle income?
faq.answer: Common deductions: vehicle mileage (67¢/mile in 2024), home office, phone/internet, supplies, software, professional development, and health insurance premiums if you qualify.

faq.question: How much should I set aside for taxes?
faq.answer: Set aside 25-35% of your net side hustle income for taxes. At the 22% bracket: ~30% covers self-employment tax + income tax. Pay quarterly estimated taxes to avoid penalties.

formulas: Net Income = Gross Income - Business Expenses
Income Tax = (Net - SE Deduction) × Tax Bracket%
Take-Home = Net - SE Tax - Income Tax

tagline: Break down your wedding budget by category with recommended percentages
keywords: wedding budget, wedding cost, wedding planner, wedding breakdown, wedding expenses

faq.question: How much does the average wedding cost?
faq.answer: The average US wedding costs about $30,000-$35,000 (2024). This varies enormously by region: $20,000 in rural areas to $75,000+ in major cities. Your guest count is the single biggest cost driver.

faq.question: What is the biggest wedding expense?
faq.answer: Venue and catering typically consume 40-50% of the budget. At $150-$250 per guest for food and drinks alone, a 150-person wedding easily reaches $22,500-$37,500 just for dining.

faq.question: How can I save money on a wedding?
faq.answer: Biggest savings: choose an off-peak day (Friday/Sunday), limit guest count, book in off-season (Nov-March), use a non-traditional venue, DIY flowers/centerpieces, and skip the full open bar.
